title: Littorella uniflora
taxonomy: Littorella uniflora, also known as shore line bladderwort, is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is a perennial aquatic plant that grows in shallow water with its leaves floating on the surface. The classification of Littorella uniflora is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Magnoliophyta, the class Magnoliopsida, the order Lamiales, the family Plantaginaceae, the genus Littorella, and the species L. uniflora. This classification is based on its physical characteristics and genetic makeup. The classification of Littorella uniflora is subject to ongoing research and refinement in the field of botany.

preparations: Littorella uniflora, also known as shore line plantain or money plant, has been used in traditional herbal medicine, particularly in Asian cultures. intro: Littorella uniflora, commonly known as shore dock or one-flowered water dock, is a small, aquatic plant species belonging to the family Haloragaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Littorella uniflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Littorella uniflora is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is commonly used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory infections.
